title: Festuca syrtica
taxonomy: Festuca syrtica is classified under the kingdom Plantae, due to being a type of plant. It belongs to the division Magnoliophyta, which includes flowering plants. This species is further classified under the class Liliopsida, a group of monocotyledonous flowering plants. It is then placed under the order Poales, a group of flowering plants with a single cotyledon. Festuca syrtica is finally classified under the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family.

intro: Festuca syrtica, also known as Festuca grass, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Festuca syrtica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Festuca syrtica is often prepared as a decoction or tea, as well as being used in combination with other herbs in various tinctures and infusions to support respiratory health and boost the immune system.
